pr.title: mn: Implement Vibration menu initialization and formalize Count logic
pr.author: Subarasheese

summary: Implemented previously skeletal menu code for the Vibration menu initialization and added concrete Count-menu ranking/tie-resolution logic. The PR populated src/melee/mn/mnvibration.c with mnVibration_80249174 asset loading, menu-state setup, GObj/proc creation, and a register-sensitive call workaround. It also implemented mnCount_8025035C and mnCount_8025072C, formalized their prototypes, added CountEntry to mn/types.h, and preserved placeholder prototypes after review feedback. Matching notes in the PR context report fuzzy matches of 78.15% for mnVibration_80249174, 99.71% for mnCount_8025035C, and 64.49% for mnCount_8025072C.
what_changed: Added mnVibration_80249174 in src/melee/mn/mnvibration.c, including menu-state initialization of mn_804D6BC8.cooldown, mn_804A04F0.prev_menu, mn_804A04F0.cur_menu = 19, and mn_804A04F0.hovered_selection.
Added MnVibrationAssets local helper struct and global mnVibration_804A0868 to hold Vibration menu joint, animjoint, matanim_joint, and shapeanim_joint asset pointers.
Loaded Vibration menu assets with lbArchive_LoadSections using strings such as MnVibTitle_Top_joint, MnVibSCl_Top_joint, MnVibOption_Top_joint, and MnVibPanel_Top_joint.
Created a GObj via GObj_Create and attached fn_80247510 as a process through HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 in mnVibration_80249174.
Adjusted a GObj flag byte at offset 0xD using HSD_GObj_804D783C shifted into bits masked by 0x30.

Implemented mnCount_8025035C as a stable selection-sort-like routine over a 25-entry CountEntry stack array, ranking fighter IDs by a callback-provided stat and returning 25 as an invalid/no-result sentinel.
Implemented mnCount_8025035C_inline to scan persistent fighter data for a field at offset 0x54 and early-return true when no entries have that field set.

Implemented mnCount_8025072C to resolve ties among CountEntry records beginning at start_idx, using GetFighterTotalKOs as a secondary stat and GetFighterTotalFalls as an additional tie-breaker, with behavior dependent on mode.

Changed mncount.h header guard from MELEE_MN_COUNT_H to MELEE_MN_MNCOUNT_H and added typed prototypes for mnCount_8025035C and mnCount_8025072C while retaining placeholder prototypes for other functions.
Moved CountEntry into src/melee/mn/types.h after review feedback and added a CountEntry forward declaration to src/melee/mn/forward.h.
Tidied forward declarations in src/melee/mn/forward.h, including adding AnimLoopSettings and CountEntry and alphabetizing/reordering menu-related forward declarations.
smart_moves: Used ARRAY_SIZE(sp18) to replace repeated hard-coded 25 loop bounds in mnCount_8025035C after review feedback, making the stack-array logic safer and easier to maintain.
Used PAD_STACK(4) instead of a dummy stack_pad local for stack-shape control, matching project style for intentional padding.
Kept placeholders in mncount.h for unimplemented functions to preserve function order and satisfy --require-protos, while still giving concrete prototypes to newly implemented functions.
Placed CountEntry in mn/types.h rather than only in mncount.h so it can be shared through the mn type system and forward-declared cleanly.
Used bool, true, and false in the inline helper after review feedback instead of BOOL and integer boolean literals.
Preserved stable ordering in mnCount_8025035C by shifting entries when moving the selected best entry, rather than performing a simple swap, which matters for tie behavior.
The Vibration asset loading order was deliberately arranged as Title, SCl, Option, Panel to match original register scheduling according to the PR context.
Used function pointer casts around calls such as mnVibration_80248ED4 and fn_80247510 where signatures or headers were not yet aligned, as a local matching workaround rather than broad header churn.
decomp_lessons: Do not remove UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholder prototypes from partially decompiled headers just because some functions are now typed; they maintain address order and keep --require-protos satisfied.
For menu structs used across multiple mn files, prefer shared definitions in mn/types.h plus forward declarations in mn/forward.h over local header-only definitions.
When a count/ranking routine returns a sentinel for unresolved ties, preserve the observed sentinel value. In this PR, 25 is used both as the array size and invalid/no-result return.
Stable selection-sort behavior can be important for matching and semantics; moving the best element by shifting intervening entries is not equivalent to swapping if original order must be preserved.
Use ARRAY_SIZE for fixed local arrays once the array object exists; it documents the relationship between loop bounds, return sentinel, and stack storage.
Use project stack-padding macros such as PAD_STACK(4) or FORCE_PAD_STACK_4 rather than artificial locals when shaping stack frames for matching.
Prefer standard lowercase bool/true/false in new C code when the surrounding project style supports it.
Asset-loading vararg order can affect register allocation and matching; keep lbArchive_LoadSections string/pointer ordering close to the original scheduling instead of grouping purely by readability.

Function pointer casts can be an acceptable narrow workaround for signature/header mismatches during decomp, but the evidence here frames them as matching workarounds, not a final type-system solution.
naming_conventions: Menu functions follow the subsystem/address naming style, e.g. mnVibration_80249174, mnCount_8025035C, and mnCount_8025072C.
Local helper structs used before full reverse-engineered names are available used descriptive but provisional names such as MnVibrationAssets and LocalFighterData.
Shared menu data structs use PascalCase typedef/struct names such as CountEntry, CSSData, CSSIcon, and MenuKindData.
CountEntry fields are short and layout-driven: id, pad, and val.
The updated header guard for mncount.h follows the path-qualified pattern MELEE_MN_MNCOUNT_H.
Asset fields and strings mirror archive symbol names, e.g. Title_Top_joint corresponds to MnVibTitle_Top_joint, and Option_Top_animjoint corresponds to MnVibOption_Top_animjoint.
Reviewer feedback favored project-standard bool/true/false naming over BOOL and numeric 0/1 boolean returns.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Reported fuzzy match quality was 78.15% for mnVibration_80249174, 99.71% for mnCount_8025035C, and 64.49% for mnCount_8025072C.
mnCount_8025035C used PAD_STACK(4) to shape the stack frame.
mnCount_8025035C used a local CountEntry sp18[25] and ARRAY_SIZE(sp18), with arr_size also serving as the 25 sentinel return.
mnCount_8025035C used a stable selection-sort implementation with an insertion-style shift to preserve order while selecting descending values.
Callback calls in mnCount_8025035C explicitly cast fighter IDs to s8 before invoking u32 (*get_val_func)(s8), matching the intended argument type.
mnVibration_80249174 used a function pointer cast to call mnVibration_80248ED4 as void (*)(int), described in the PR context as a workaround for header/signature mismatch and register allocation.
mnVibration_80249174 cast fn_80247510 to void (*)(HSD_GObj*) for HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54.
mnVibration_80249174 manually edited the GObj flag byte at offset 0xD with mask 0xCF and value derived from HSD_GObj_804D783C << 4, preserving low-level flag behavior.
lbArchive_LoadSections varargs were ordered to match original scheduling: Title assets first, then SCl, Option, and Panel.

review_feedback.feedback: Do not remove placeholder prototypes; they keep function order and satisfy --require-protos.
review_feedback.outcome: Final diff retains placeholder prototypes for unimplemented mnCount functions while adding concrete prototypes for mnCount_8025035C and mnCount_8025072C.

review_feedback.feedback: Use ARRAY_SIZE(sp18) in place of hard-coded 25 instances.
review_feedback.outcome: Final diff defines ssize_t const arr_size = ARRAY_SIZE(sp18) and uses arr_size in loops and sentinel returns inside mnCount_8025035C.
Final diff adds struct CountEntry to src/melee/mn/types.h and only forward-declares CountEntry in forward.h.

review_feedback.feedback: Use PAD_STACK(4), or FORCE_PAD_STACK_4 if that does not work, instead of a dummy stack local.
review_feedback.outcome: Final diff uses PAD_STACK(4) at the start of mnCount_8025035C.

review_feedback.feedback: Use bool, true, and false rather than all-caps BOOL and numeric boolean literals.
review_feedback.outcome: Final diff changes mnCount_8025035C_inline to return bool and uses true/false.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that improve the 78.15% fuzzy match for mnVibration_80249174.
Find later PRs that replace the function pointer cast around mnVibration_80248ED4 with a correct prototype.
Find later PRs that improve or fully match mnCount_8025072C tie-breaking logic.
Search for other uses of CountEntry after it was moved into mn/types.h.
Search for other menu files that use PAD_STACK or FORCE_PAD_STACK_4 for matching.
Check whether LocalFighterData offset 0x54 was later formalized into a named persistent fighter data struct field.
